questions.question: On which Notion plans is the Google Drive AI Connector available?

options.explanation: The connector is not offered on free or personal plans.
Only Business and Enterprise plans include the Google Drive AI Connector.
Education plans are not mentioned as supporting this feature.
The feature is limited to higher‑tier plans, not all plans.

questions.question: Who must be both a Google Drive admin and a Notion workspace owner to initiate the connection?

options.text: Any member of the Notion workspace
options.explanation: Only a user with both admin and ownership roles can start the connection.

options.text: A Google Drive admin who is also the Notion workspace owner
options.explanation: Both conditions are required for the initial setup.
The Notion admin also needs Google Drive admin rights.
Super‑admin is not sufficient; the user must also own the Notion workspace.

questions.question: How long can the initial Google Drive‑Notion AI integration take to complete?

options.explanation: The documentation states the connection may take up to 36 hours.
The sync is far longer than a few minutes.
While possible, the maximum specified is 36 hours.
The stated maximum is 36 hours, not 72.

questions.question: Which of the following file types is NOT supported by the Google Drive AI Connector?

options.explanation: Google Docs are explicitly supported.
PDFs are listed among supported uploaded file types.
.xlsx files are supported as uploaded files.
Video files like .mp4 are not mentioned as supported content types.

questions.question: A file is shared with "Anyone with link" but you have never opened it. Will it appear in Notion AI search results?

options.text: Yes, immediately after it is shared
options.explanation: The file must be visited at least once before it becomes searchable.

options.text: No, it will never be searchable
options.explanation: It can become searchable after you visit it.

options.text: Yes, after you have visited the link at least once
options.explanation: The connector requires a prior visit to index "anyone with link" files.
Visiting the file is the gating condition, not admin status.

questions.question: During installation in the Google Marketplace, which setting is recommended for the connector?

options.explanation: Limiting to yourself would prevent other users from accessing the connector.

options.text: Install for All users in the organization
options.explanation: The article recommends installing for all users so everyone can use the connector.
While possible, it is not the recommended default.

options.text: Do not install; use a manual API token
options.explanation: The connector relies on the Marketplace installation.

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes Notion AI's handling of Google Drive permissions?

options.text: Notion AI can ignore Google Drive permissions and show all files to every user
options.explanation: Notion AI respects existing Drive permissions.

options.text: Notion AI respects Google Drive permissions for both individual users and shared access types
options.explanation: The connector maps Drive permissions to Notion users, honoring restrictions.

options.text: Only files owned by the connector initiator are searchable
options.explanation: Ownership by selected groups or users also grants access.

options.text: Permissions are overridden so that all shared drives are always searchable
options.explanation: Permissions are not overridden; excluded drives remain hidden.

questions.question: After the initial connection, how frequently does newly added content in Google Docs become searchable through Notion AI?

options.explanation: The sync interval is longer than a minute.
New content is indexed approximately every hour.
The article specifies an hourly update, not daily.
There is a short delay; it is not instant.

questions.question: A company wants a non‑super‑admin to initiate the Google Drive connection. Which set of privileges must be granted to that role?

options.text: Users → Select Read; Services → Drives and Docs → Settings; Google Workspace Marketplace → Settings
options.explanation: Missing several required privileges such as group selection and security center rights.

options.text: Users → Select Read; Users → Select Groups; Services → Drives and Docs → Settings; Services → Security Center → Activity Rules → Manage & View; Google Workspace Marketplace → Settings; Admin API: Reports → Read, Groups → Select, Organization Units → Read
options.explanation: This combination matches the detailed list of privileges needed for a role without super‑admin rights.
Far too limited; additional Drive and user/group privileges are required.

options.text: Users → Select Groups; Services → Drives and Docs → Settings; Reports → Manager customer → Read
options.explanation: Missing essential read permissions for users and other admin API privileges.

questions.question: Why will a file owned by a user in an excluded organizational unit not be ingested, even if the shared drive containing the file is included in the connector settings?

options.text: Because Notion AI only checks the shared‑drive inclusion and ignores user ownership.
options.explanation: Both shared‑drive inclusion and user ownership are required for ingestion.

options.text: Because the connector requires both the file to be owned by a selected user/group *and* the drive to be selected; exclusion of the org unit removes the user from the allowed set, so the file fails the ownership condition.
options.explanation: The file is blocked because the owner’s org unit is excluded, breaking the ownership rule even though the drive is allowed.

options.text: Because shared drives automatically override any organizational‑unit exclusions.
options.explanation: Shared‑drive overrides do not apply to ownership checks; exclusions still apply.

options.text: Because Notion AI treats all files in excluded org units as deleted.
options.explanation: Files are not deleted; they are simply not ingested due to permission mapping.
